calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-14-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Enforcing Liens
Off Calendar May-14-2019 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-12-2019 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jun-18-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For Leave To Complete Discovery Closer To Trial Date
Off Calendar Mar-27-2019 Advanced From Apr-16-19 To Apr-05-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-12-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion For Leave To Complete Discovery Closer To Trial Date
Off Calendar Apr-05-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christopher Nolin, Esq. 415-421-3100 for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LOOP TRANSPORTATION, INC., JASON ATKINSON's Motion For Leave To Complete Discovery Closer To Trial Date.

Defendants' motion is GRANTED. Defendants may obtain the records called for by the outstanding subpoenas identified in Defendants' motion (the "Records"), subject to the following limitations and conditions:
1. Defendants may retain those Records, if any, relating to the examination or treatment of any part of Plaintiff's neck or head from 2011 onwards;
2. Plaintiff may designate as "Privileged" those parts of the Records, if any, which do not relate to the examination or treatment of any part of Plaintiff's neck or head from 2011 onwards;
3. Defendants may challenge Plaintiff's designation(s) of privilege, if any, in which event Plaintiff shall have the burden of establishing the applicability of the designation;
4. Defendants shall redact or destroy as appropriate any Records that Plaintiff designates as privileged, except to the extent they successfully challenge such designation; and
5. Defendants must act with all diligence following receipt of the Records to make their experts available for deposition prior to eliciting opinion evidence from them at trial.

Plaintiff's motion is DENIED in view of the above.

An order is signed for both motions. (see scanned order for details)

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Vestal, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoenas And Motion For A Protective Order-Hrg 04-05-19
Off Calendar Apr-05-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christopher Nolin, Esq. 415-421-3100 for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DREW KLEIN's Motion To Quash Subpoenas And Motion For A Protective Order (Hrg 04-05-19.)

Defendants' motion is GRANTED. Defendants may obtain the records called for by the outstanding subpoenas identified in Defendants' motion (the "Records"), subject to the following limitations and conditions:
1. Defendants may retain those Records, if any, relating to the examination or treatment of any part of Plaintiff's neck or head from 2011 onwards;
2. Plaintiff may designate as "Privileged" those parts of the Records, if any, which do not relate to the examination or treatment of any part of Plaintiff's neck or head from 2011 onwards;
3. Defendants may challenge Plaintiff's designation(s) of privilege, if any, in which event Plaintiff shall have the burden of establishing the applicability of the designation;
4. Defendants shall redact or destroy as appropriate any Records that Plaintiff designates as privileged, except to the extent they successfully challenge such designation; and
5. Defendants must act with all diligence following receipt of the Records to make their experts available for deposition prior to eliciting opinion evidence from them at trial.

Plaintiff's motion is DENIED in view of the above.

An order is signed for both motions. (see scanned order for details)

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Vestal, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
